market_summary: US markets closed for Memorial Day weekend (Sat-Mon). Friday close: S&P 500 +0.4% (8th consecutive weekly gain, longest since Dec 2023), Dow +0.58% to 50,580 (ATH), Nasdaq +0.2%. Risk-on driven by Iran ceasefire extension optimism — FT reports US and Iran 'closing in' on 60-day ceasefire extension with nuclear framework. Bond selloff continues: 10Y 4.57%, 30Y 5.06-5.10%. DXY 99.3 (flat week). Brent ~$104.5, WTI ~$99.5. BTC fell to $74.3-75.6K. MSTR -3.0% to $159.89. Gold $4,519-4,521. Silver $75.35-76.24.
key_events_next_week: Mon May 25: US CLOSED (Memorial Day). Crypto/FX/metals trade thin liquidity.
Tue May 26: US markets reopen. Consumer Confidence (Conference Board, 10AM ET). New Residential Sales. Richmond Fed Manufacturing Survey.
Wed May 27: No major US releases scheduled. Corporate Bond Market Distress Index.
Thu May 28: GDP Q1 2nd estimate, Durable Goods Orders, Initial Claims, PCE Deflator, Personal Income & Spending — HEAVY DATA DAY.
Fri May 29: Chicago PMI, Advanced Trade/Retail/Wholesale Inventories. Shangri-La Dialogue opens in Singapore (defense summit, May 29-31).
Sat May 30: Shangri-La Dialogue continues — potential Asia geopolitical headlines.
NO FOMC this week (next: Jun 16-17). NO CPI (next: Jun 10). NO NFP (next: Jun 5).
overnight_risks: Iran deal momentum: FT reports US-Iran 'closing in' on 60-day ceasefire extension + nuclear framework. Trump: 'getting a lot closer.' Breakdown = Brent $114+, gold spike, BTC selloff. Deal = risk-on, oil -$10, gold dip, equities gap up Tuesday.
Strait of Hormuz: Gradual reopening in proposed deal. Still effectively closed — 10M+ bbl/day disrupted. Any incident = oil shock.
Weekend crypto vulnerability: BTC at $74-75K, near key $74K support. Thin weekend liquidity amplifies moves. Fear & Greed at 29 (Fear). 57% of technicals bearish.

Asia defense posture: US redeployed THAAD/Patriot from South Korea + 2500 marines from Japan to Middle East. Beijing assertiveness risk. Shangri-La Dialogue May 29-31 could surface Taiwan/SCS tensions.
MSTR gap risk: BTC weekend move → MSTR gap Tuesday. At $159.89, already -3% Friday.
Qatar LNG disruption: Iranian attack on Ras Laffan reduced Qatar LNG exports by ~17%. Energy supply shock persists.

instrument_news.XAU-USD: Gold $4,519-4,521, range-bound. 30Y at 5.06-5.10% creating headwind via real yields. Central bank buying surged to 243t in Q1 2026 (+35% QoQ from 207t Q4 2025), running ~585t/quarter annualized. JPM $5,000 Q4 target intact. ETF flows mixed: US gold ETFs +$0.83B April but persistent outflows since Feb/Mar. COT: net non-commercial positioning largely unchanged since early Feb, producers increasing hedging. Iran deal = short-term dip risk (risk-on reduces haven bid). Support $4,400-4,460, resistance $4,580-4,590.
instrument_news.XAG-USD: Silver $75.35-76.24, down 1.69% Friday. Gold/silver ratio ~56-63 (compressed). Sixth consecutive supply deficit year (46.3 Moz). Solar PV substitution headwind at $80+. COT: specs net long 26,111 contracts (room for more). Patient HOLD position at 2.6oz @ ~$75.57 is near breakeven. Support $75 critical, resistance $78-83. Industrial demand intact but macro headwinds from DXY and yields.
instrument_news.BTC-USD: BTC $74,326-75,580, down ~1.5% Friday. ETF outflows: record $635M single-day outflow May 13, ~$1.15B outflows this week. BUT: exchange reserves at 7-year lows, whales net-bought 270K BTC recently, large holders (10-10K BTC) accumulated 61,568 BTC in past month. Mining 450/day vs ETF demand 4,500-5,000/day when positive. Fear & Greed 29 (Fear). BTC dominance 60%. CPI 3.8% April (highest since Sep 2023) pushing back rate cut expectations. Coiled spring thesis intact but near-term bearish. Support $74K critical, resistance $78K-80K.
instrument_news.ETH-USD: ETH $2,029-2,118, range-bound. Market cap $255B (#2). 35.8M ETH staked (30% supply). DeFi TVL $45.74B. Glamsterdam upgrade June 2026 catalyst (3x L1 throughput). Altcoin Season Index 39/100 = 'Bitcoin Season', ETH underperforming. Trading volume $6.9-10.1B/24h. Support $2,050, resistance $2,200-2,400.
instrument_news.MSTR: MSTR $159.89, down 3.0% Friday (range $158.61-165.75). Holds 818,334 BTC (~$60B at current BTC price). Recent purchase: 22,337 BTC funded via STRC preferred shares. Trades as 1.5-2x leveraged BTC proxy. BTC weekend decline = MSTR gap down Tuesday. Support $155-158, resistance $165-170. Q1 2026 earnings already reported — focus now on BTC correlation.

metals_outlook: Structural bull intact but near-term consolidation. Central bank buying accelerated to 243t Q1 (+35% QoQ) — strongest quarterly demand in years. Silver supply deficit persistent (6th year). Headwinds: 30Y yields >5%, DXY stable at 99.3, persistent ETF outflows. Iran deal progress = risk-on = temporary gold/silver dip (safe haven unwind). No deal = renewed haven bid. Gold rangebound $4,400-4,590. Silver critical at $75 support — Patient position at $75.57 is right at the line. COT positioning not extreme in either direction — room for moves both ways. Thursday's PCE deflator is the key data point for metals this week.
oil_outlook: Brent ~$104.5, WTI ~$99.5. Strait of Hormuz effectively closed — 10M+ bbl/day disrupted (~20% global supply). EIA projects 8.5M b/d global stock decline Q2. OPEC+ modest increases 188-206K bbl/day for May-June. Qatar LNG -17% from Iranian attack. Iran deal = oil -$8-12 on Hormuz reopening expectations. No deal = Brent $114+ retest. UAE departed OPEC May 1 adds uncertainty. US refinery utilization 92%. Oil is THE binary trade on Iran deal outcome this weekend.
crypto_outlook: Bearish near-term, constructive medium-term. BTC: ETF outflows accelerating, CPI 3.8% kills rate cut hopes, Fear at 29, 57% technicals bearish. BUT: on-chain accumulation massive (exchange reserves 7yr low, 270K whale buys), supply squeeze building. ETH: Glamsterdam June catalyst but underperforming in Bitcoin Season (dominance 60%). Weekend risk: thin liquidity, Iran headline binary. If BTC breaks $74K support, cascading liquidations possible. If Iran deal announced = risk-on = BTC could snap to $78K+. Key level: $74K BTC support must hold.
sentiment_shift: Market pricing Iran deal as base case (equities at ATH, Dow 50,580). FT headline 'closing in on 60-day ceasefire' is bullish. BUT: Trump rejected Iran's latest proposal — deal not done. Consensus too complacent on deal risk. Bond market not buying it — 30Y >5%, 2Y above FFR. FOMC 8-4 split (historic). Fed chair transition (Powell term ending) adds uncertainty. Thursday May 28 is the big data day: GDP revision + PCE deflator + durable goods will set tone for June FOMC.

treasury_yields.2Y: 4.14% — above Fed funds rate (3.50-3.75%), market pricing no near-term cuts
treasury_yields.10Y: 4.57% — steady, rose to 4.62% Thursday before settling
treasury_yields.30Y: 5.06-5.10% — near highest since 2007, headwind for gold

dxy: 99.3 — flat on the week, trading in tight range. Iran deal progress keeping it from breaking higher. Modest headwind for metals/crypto at current levels.
fed: 3.50-3.75% hold. 8-4 split (largest in 30+ years). Powell term ending May 2026. Market pricing 40% chance December hike. CPI 3.8% April complicates outlook. Next FOMC Jun 16-17.
